title: Add CKC media row-level attribution and parallel-agent conflict safety
operator_request: Capture the parallel-agent attribution risk found during MT-010: event headers are not enough if album rows and media links do not show who created, linked, edited, or removed them.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by adding persisted row-level attribution where missing. Do not treat HTTP headers alone as sufficient for later no-context model recovery.

scope.summary: Persist and surface actor attribution on CKC albums, media membership links, image-note edits, and album mutations so multiple agents can work safely and recovery can explain who changed what.
base_scope: Audit existing album/media storage rows for created_by, linked_by, updated_by, removed_by, and timestamps.
Add migrations/storage fields where attribution is missing and safe to add.
Populate row-level attribution from x-hsk-actor-id or the CKC media actor field on create/link/edit/unlink/reorder operations.
Expose attribution in backend responses and frontend Argus-readable row labels or detail readouts.
Add conflict/retry behavior or explicit last-writer status where two agents edit the same link/member.
acceptance_criteria: Album create responses include persisted creator attribution.
Album membership/link responses include persisted linked_by or equivalent attribution for each member occurrence.
Image notes/tags save responses expose who last edited the notes/tags where storage supports it.
Parallel writes by two actor IDs leave inspectable attribution that distinguishes them.
No migration or response shape silently breaks existing frontend parsing.
risks_and_controls: Risk: attribution exists only in event logs and is lost during state recovery. Control: row-level response tests assert actor fields.
Risk: migration breaks existing data. Control: nullable/defaulted fields and migration smoke tests.
Risk: parallel updates overwrite each other silently. Control: visible last-writer or conflict status and actor-specific tests.
verification: Backend persisted attribution tests for album and link rows.
Frontend parser/render tests for attribution readouts.
Parallel actor scenario using two distinct actor IDs.

manual_requirement: Required if attribution becomes operator/model visible.

handoff.note: Created from MT-010 review finding that event/header attribution alone is insufficient for row-level media recovery.
